title: Missing zero address validation for fee recipient
reason: This is an admin misconfiguration risk only. Setting `feeTo` to `address(0)` burns protocol fees but does not create a permissionless exploit or user-funds vulnerability in the audited logic.

title: Potential integer overflow in exchange rate calculation
reason: The contract is compiled with Solidity 0.8.10, so arithmetic overflow reverts automatically. This is not a live vulnerability.

title: Permissionless liquidation can be triggered by anyone
reason: `unstakeAndRepay` is intentionally public and pays a liquidation reward. It is gated by a health check on the victim position, so this is expected protocol behavior rather than an access-control flaw.

title: Unlimited ERC20 token approvals
reason: Granting max approvals to trusted core integrations is standard and not reportable on its own without a concrete compromise or malicious-upgrade path in scope.

title: Missing input validation for NFT asset in staking
reason: `depositAndBorrowApeAndStake` immediately calls `_getPTokenStaking(stakingInfo.nftAsset)`, which already restricts `nftAsset` to BAYC or MAYC via `require`.

title: Missing array length validation in withdraw function
reason: Supplying very large arrays only risks the caller's own gas usage. This is not a protocol-level vulnerability.

title: Missing validation for stakingInfo borrowAmount
reason: Borrow sizing is already constrained by the staking-rate check in ApeStaking and by downstream `apePool.borrowBehalf` logic. The candidate does not identify a concrete bypass.

title: No check for duplicate NFT IDs in deposit arrays
reason: Duplicate IDs would cause the second `safeTransferFrom` to fail because the caller no longer owns the NFT, reverting the transaction rather than creating exploitable incorrect accounting.

title: No access control on claimAndRestake
reason: The function does have access control: `msg.sender` must be `userAddr` or hold `REINVEST_ROLE`. The reported title is factually incorrect.

title: Division by zero in getStakeInfo when poolId is invalid
reason: `getStakeInfo` performs no division at all. The candidate is unsupported by the source code.
